comment: Single source of truth for model-credential forwarding across the Python worker (apps/worker/src/agentos_worker/sandbox/docker.py) and the Rust CLI (cli/src/commands.rs::select_passthrough_env). Changing a rule here requires changing both lanes; changing a lane without changing this file fails that lane's test. Read by apps/worker/tests/sandbox/test_vector_credential_forwarding.py and by the vector loop in cli/src/commands.rs's test module. Five of the six inputs are presence booleans; the rule keys on a credential's content in exactly one case: `byo_oauth_shaped` marks a BYO credential as an OAuth-shaped Claude Code token (sk-ant-oat prefix, per runner/src/agentos_runner/sdk_auth.py::OAUTH_TOKEN_PREFIX), which under a base-URL override authenticates against nothing and is dropped so a real token never sits inert in a container that resolves none (issue #603). expected is an ordered list of env NAMES; both lanes assert order. Both lanes reject unknown fields, so adding an input here without teaching both lanes fails both: a key one lane cannot see would otherwise pass vacuously.

vectors.name: fake_model_forwards_nothing_even_with_byo
vectors.why: A fake-model runner authenticates nothing, so an explicit BYO credential must not sit in it.

vectors.name: fake_model_forwards_nothing_with_ambient_only
vectors.why: A fully-credentialed host must not leak its ambient SDK tokens into a fake-model runner.

vectors.name: fake_model_dominates_base_url_override
vectors.why: Fake is the dominant state: it suppresses even the BYO credential an override alone would keep.

vectors.name: explicit_byo_forwarded_alone
vectors.why: The operator's chosen BYO credential is forwarded alone so an ambient token cannot shadow it or ride along.

vectors.name: base_url_override_keeps_explicit_byo
vectors.why: The runner routes an sk-or- OpenRouter provider key into ANTHROPIC_API_KEY even with a preset base URL; suppressing it would break BYO OpenRouter. A provider key is not OAuth-shaped, so the override drop does not apply.

vectors.name: base_url_override_keeps_explicit_byo_without_ambient
vectors.why: The BYO provider key under an override does not depend on any ambient token being present.

vectors.name: base_url_override_drops_oauth_shaped_byo
vectors.why: An sk-ant-oat OAuth token under a base-URL override authenticates against nothing (the runner blanks it), so forwarding it only lands a real token in /proc/1/environ of a container that resolves none. Drop it (issue #603), unlike a provider key.

vectors.name: base_url_override_drops_oauth_shaped_byo_without_ambient
vectors.why: The OAuth-shaped drop under an override does not depend on any ambient token being present; the override run forwards nothing.

vectors.name: oauth_shaped_byo_forwarded_without_override
vectors.why: The OAuth-shape drop is gated on the base-URL override: on the legacy real-Anthropic path an sk-ant-oat token is a valid credential the runner routes into CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, so it is still forwarded.

vectors.name: base_url_override_drops_ambient_fallback
vectors.why: A local endpoint needs no real Anthropic token, so the ambient SDK fallback is dropped under an override.

vectors.name: base_url_override_with_nothing_forwards_nothing
vectors.why: An override run with no credential of any kind forwards nothing.

vectors.name: ambient_fallback_forwards_both_in_order
vectors.why: The legacy real-Anthropic path forwards the ambient SDK credentials, OAuth token before API key.

vectors.name: ambient_fallback_forwards_only_present_oauth
vectors.why: Ambient names are gated on actual presence, so an absent API key is not forwarded.

vectors.name: ambient_fallback_forwards_only_present_api_key
vectors.why: Ambient names are gated on actual presence, so an absent OAuth token is not forwarded.

vectors.name: no_credential_anywhere_forwards_nothing
vectors.why: A real-model run on an uncredentialed host forwards nothing rather than a name with no value behind it.
